documentation.readme: SysWhispers helps with evasion by generating header/ASM files implants can use to make direct system calls.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'any' phase against surfaces [web] in the c2 category. Fits when the engagement needs: SysWhispers helps with evasion by generating header/ASM files implants can use to make direct system calls.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: SysWhispers helps with evasion by generating header/ASM files implants can use to make direct system calls.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `py .\syswhispers.py --help`. Further invocations from upstream: `py .\syswhispers.py --preset all -o syscalls_all`; `py .\syswhispers.py --preset common -o syscalls_common`. SysWhispers helps with evasion by generating header/ASM files implants can use to make direct system calls.

name: SysWhispers2
summary: SysWhispers helps with evasion by generating header/ASM files implants can use to make direct system calls.

trust.warning: Attribution / index entry only. The operator must audit the code, the README, the license, and the releases before invoking. Phase 5 — entry is auto-generated from the operator's curated fetch list; no automated enrichment yet.

use_cases: When you need c2, use SysWhispers2 because SysWhispers helps with evasion by generating header/ASM files implants can use to make direct system calls.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `py .\syswhispers.py --help` as documented upstream for SysWhispers2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `py .\syswhispers.py --preset all -o syscalls_all` as documented upstream for SysWhispers2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `py .\syswhispers.py --preset common -o syscalls_common` as documented upstream for SysWhispers2.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `py .\syswhispers.py --functions NtProtectVirtualMemory,NtWriteVirtualMemory -o syscalls_mem` as documented upstream for SysWhispers2.
When chaining c2 tools, pair SysWhispers2 with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `SysWhispers2 --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
